summary: www.acuenta.cl - Slow page load, becomes unresponsive

Firefox version: Firefox 140.0/142

summary: www.keter.com - Contact form doesn't load
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. Visit https://www.keter.com/fr-fr/nous-contacter.html
2. Wait.

Actual results:
In Firefox, the page never finishes loading, and the contact form is never displayed. Only the logo placeholder is visible.

Expected results:
A contact form is displayed.

Additional context:
Reproduces in Firefox on the latest release (v149) and Nightly (v151) regardless of ETP status. Reproduces in Firefox for Android. Does NOT reproduce in Edge (desktop) or Chrome (Android).

description:

> This browser isn’t supported.
> 
> Supported Browsers:
> Google Chrome | Microsoft Edge

Another of their proprerties—https://camtasia.techsmith.com/app—(requires login) also reports "Unsupported browser" (although it lists Safari as supported) but doesn't work even with Chrome Mask, i.e. still shows unsupported browser.

summary: CIBC online video meetings reject Firefox
description:
I had an actual meeting with valid ID earlier today with CIBC and I could only use Chrome to attend it. It seems it specifies the same message without a meeting ID as it does with, so hopefully that is sufficient to debug this.

Visit https://collaboration.cibc.com/. Firefox gets an unsupported page, Chrome gets an invalid meeting page (but went to the actual meeting for me). Not sure what it is using to feature detect.

- Clean profile

**Steps to reproduce:**
1. Navigate to: https://pixi.live/gallery/opensource-experience-2025
2. Scroll down a few times and observe the images

**Expected Behavior:**
Images stop loading eventually and overlap each other

**Actual Behavior:**
Images loaded and able to scroll to the bottom of the page and few full gallery

**Notes:**
- Reproducible on the latest Firefox Release and Nightly
- Reproducible regardless of the ETP setting
- Works as expected using Chrome

---
Report: "Hello, I'm encountering a problem that only occurs in Firefox. I don't have this issue with Safari or Chrome. I'm trying to display images in a Masonry grid. When the page loads, everything is fine, but when I start scrolling and the elements load sequentially, it eventually blocks the loading of new images and breaks the layout. The images overlap. I've been able to test this, and when I set a fixed height for the images, the bug disappears. I therefore suspect an issue with variable heights and the Masonry grid. "

Created from webcompat-user-report:93f67372-ae59-420c-b168-a81f50b6910e

summary: www.americanexpress.com - "Your Global Privacy Control opt-out signal has been honored." message is displayed on every page
description:
**Steps to reproduce:**
1. Have GPC ("Tell websites not to sell or share my data") enabled.
2. Visit https://www.americanexpress.com/.
3. Browse the site, visiting different pages.

**Expected results:**
The "_Your Global Privacy Control opt-out signal has been honored._" message is displayed only once (as long as you don't change settings).

**Actual results:**
The "_Your Global Privacy Control opt-out signal has been honored._" message is displayed on every page, which is kinda irritating.

**Additional context:**
This doesn't reproduce in Chrome/Edge because they don't support GPC yet. So it's not an actual webcompat bug, rather a difference, but we might still want to take action because from an end-user perspective, this may look like a Firefox issue.
